title: Contact Sharing (Send Contact as Message Attachment)
description: Users can share a contact card (vCard-compatible) as a message attachment. The contact may be from the user's device address book OR another platform user. Recipients can view the card, save to contacts, and optionally start a conversation if the contact is a platform user.

consent_model: Sharing a device contact: user picks from address book. No background scan of address book — only explicit pick.

Sharing a platform user: target user's profile_visibility setting respected. If target set contacts_visible=false, their phone number is NEVER included in the shared card — only display_name and platform user_id.
Server strips phone number from contact payload if target_user.phone_sharing = 'nobody' or 'contacts_only' and sender is not in target's contact list.
pii_handling: Phone numbers in contact payloads encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) in message JSONB field.
No indexing of phone numbers in search indexes.
Contact payloads purged on message deletion (cascade).

contact.phone_number: string | null (E.164 format, may be null per privacy settings)
contact.platform_user_id: UUID | null (if contact is platform user)
contact.avatar_url: string | null (CDN URL, time-limited signed URL)

server_validation: If platform_user_id provided: resolve user, apply visibility checks, strip phone if needed.
Sanitize all string fields (no HTML/script injection).

ui.trigger: Attachment picker (📎) → 'Contact' option → Platform contact search OR device contacts picker
ui.platform_search: Real-time search by username/display_name within the platform. No phone number leakage in search results.

failure_handling.permission_denied: If OS-level contacts permission denied: show platform-user search only. No fallback scraping.
failure_handling.visibility_check_failure: If platform user lookup returns 404 or 403: contact card sent without platform_user_id and without phone (degraded but safe).
